definition: One still in training on the noble path, not yet fully complete.
notes: The project prefers trainee because it is direct and doctrinally precise enough without sounding stiff. One in training is acceptable in explanatory prose when the sentence needs a fuller phrase. The term should be heard as a formal path-classification: it refers to those who have entered noble training but have not yet reached the completion marked by the arahant.

context_rules.context: In explanatory prose where a fuller phrase reads more naturally
When contrasting the noble disciple still on the path with the one beyond training
context_rules.notes: Keep the technical contrast with asekha explicit.

example_phrases.notes: Useful expression for the unfinished but noble character of the stage.
